title: What Muslims believe
summary: A Muslim believes in Allah alone, His angels, His books, His messengers, the Last Day, and divine decree. This belief shapes worship, character, patience, and hope.
steps: Learn the six articles of faith.
Protect tawhid by worshiping Allah alone.
Ask Allah for certainty and sincerity.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: How to approach the five daily prayers
summary: Salah is the daily meeting with Allah. Start by praying on time, learning purification, facing the qibla, and improving recitation gradually.
steps: Pray with humility and keep learning.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Wudu basics
summary: Wudu prepares the body and heart for prayer. Wash the required limbs carefully and avoid wastefulness.
steps: Wash the face, arms, wipe head, and wash feet.
references: Bukhari and Muslim narrations on wudu
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Fasting with purpose
summary: Fasting Ramadan trains taqwa. It includes avoiding food, drink, and marital relations from dawn to sunset, while guarding the tongue and heart.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Zakah basics
summary: Zakah purifies wealth and supports eligible recipients. It has rules around thresholds, timing, and asset types.
steps: Check the nisab and one lunar year.
Ask a scholar or trusted calculator for specifics.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Preparing for pilgrimage
summary: Hajj is an obligation for those able to perform it. Umrah is a blessed visit to the Sacred House with ihram, tawaf, sa'i, and shaving or trimming.
steps: Prepare documents and health needs early.
references: Bukhari and Muslim on Hajj rites
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Marriage as worship
summary: Marriage is built on mercy, responsibility, honesty, and good character. Islam protects rights and encourages kindness between spouses.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Family ties
summary: Islam gives great weight to kindness to parents and maintaining family ties, while still requiring wisdom and justice.
steps: Set boundaries without cruelty when needed.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Good character
summary: Good character is worship in daily life: truthfulness, patience, modesty, mercy, and reliability.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Navigating halal and haram
summary: Halal and haram questions need knowledge, context, and humility. Clear matters should be followed, and doubtful matters should be treated carefully.
steps: Ask qualified people for specific cases.
references: Bukhari and Muslim on doubtful matters
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Returning to Allah
summary: Repentance means leaving the sin, regretting it, resolving not to return, and restoring rights when others were harmed. Allah loves those who repent.
steps: Replace the habit with good deeds.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Remembering the Hereafter
summary: Remembering death softens the heart, gives priorities, and reminds us to prepare with faith, repentance, and good deeds.
steps: Make dua for a good ending.
Do good before delays become regrets.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Learning from the Prophets
summary: The stories of the Prophets teach tawhid, patience, da'wah, trust in Allah, and courage during hardship.
steps: Look for the lesson, not just the event.
Connect the lesson to daily choices.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.

title: Beginning the Seerah
summary: The Seerah shows how the Prophet ﷺ lived revelation: mercy, courage, patience, worship, leadership, and care for people.
steps: Start with Makkah and Madinah overview.
disclaimer: For personal learning. Ask a qualified scholar for specific rulings.
